title: Sphaeralcea fasciculata
taxonomy: Sphaeralcea fasciculata is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Malvaceae, commonly known as the mallow or hibiscus family. This plant is a member of the genus Sphaeralcea, which consists of about 15 species native to North America. The genus Sphaeralcea is further classified within the tribe Malveae, a group of plants that are primarily characterized by their five-petaled flowers. The Malveae tribe is part of the subfamily Malvoideae, which is a division within the family Malvaceae. The classification of Sphaeralcea fasciculata reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the mallow family.
uses: Sphaeralcea fasciculata, also known as the orange globemallow, has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Its roots and flowers are said to have been used by Native Americans to treat a variety of ailments, including fever and skin irritations. The plant's roots contain compounds that may have antiseptic and antispasmodic effects, making it potentially useful for wound healing and pain relief. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Sphaeralcea fasciculata for medicinal purposes. Traditional uses should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.

The plant is rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds, which may have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. intro: Sphaeralcea fasciculata, commonly known as the orange globemallow, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and digestive issues. Bioactive constituents of Sphaeralcea fasciculata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
